+        "title": "Check current podman-container-tools setup",
+        "description": "Verify your podman-container-tools version and configuration:\n```bash\npodman --version\n```\nThis feature requires a working podman-container-tools installation."

+        "title": "Review podman-container-tools configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant podman-container-tools configuration:\n# **kind feature**\n\n**Description**\n\nDocker has a \"rename\" subcommand allowing to change a container name. This feature is missing in podman, and is useful in some cases.\n\nThank you!"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for RFE: Add \"rename\" subcommand for better Docker compatibility",
+        "description": "Aliases would work like container names for everything except PS, where\nwe'd probably need a separate column for them (would be good enough for\ngrep, but probably break things if you're parsing the output of PS). Of\ncourse, if you wanted to filter to a single container based on name/alias,\nwe could make that work exactly like it does now\n\nOn Mon, Dec 3, 2018, 11:54 Cédric Jeanneret <notifications.com wrote:\n\n>  <https://github.com/baude> I think the id can change - at least, I\n> see no reason\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"RFE: Add \"rename\" subcommand for better Docker compatibility\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Aliases would work like container names for everything except PS, where\nwe'd probably need a separate column for them (would be good enough for\ngrep, but probably break things if you're parsing the output of PS).",

+    }
